title: Cambridge: David & Debbie Ford, St John's

main_route.excerpt: Simon, Eve & Charles set off for __Cambridge__ at 10.30. We lunched in a field & arrived on David & Debbie Ford's drive at 10.30.

stops.excerpt: David took Charles, Rachael & Daniel to Mid Summer Common fair
Simon & Eve went to the Master's Tea in St. John's garden. It was very pretty.
Charles & Eve set off for St. John's College Chapel at 9.40. Just before we left Simon arrived to walk in with us.
had lunch at the Grad Pad

unresolved.issue: A person called on at Cambridge; location within Cambridge not specified and not independently verifiable
unresolved.excerpt: Afterwards we went to call on Mrs. Bambrough

unresolved.issue: Person visited in Cambridge; address not given. Could be the same Richard mentioned as having a bike accident — context places him in Cambridge but exact location unknown
unresolved.excerpt: we called on Richard Holroyd, where we met James & Barbara Ko[?ndell]

boundary_flags: Page A_0268_R is a mixed-start page: the upper portion belongs to a preceding Wales trip (Portmeirion, Shrewsbury); the Cambridge trip begins at 'Saturday June 26th' on that same page.
summary: Simon, Eve and Charles made a two-day visit to friends David and Debbie Ford in Cambridge on 26-27 June 1999. They spent the Saturday evening at the Fords, attended a barbeque, and on Sunday visited St John's College Chapel, Midsummer Common fair and lunched at the Grad Pad before driving home to Birmingham.
extraction_notes: Only Simon, Eve and Charles travelled on this trip (the other sons are not mentioned). The diary note 'arrived on David & Debbie Ford's drive at 10.30' appears to repeat the departure time (also 10.30); this is likely a diary slip — the arrival was evidently in the evening given they 'lunched in a field' en route. Simon also spent the night of the 26th in St. John's College after the college dinner, while Eve and Charles slept on the Ford's drive. The page A_0269_R is also a mixed page: it begins with a Wales May 1999 photo caption before continuing the Cambridge narrative. No ferry or international crossing involved — entirely domestic UK trip.
